title: Spicy Crispy Tunisian Banana Tagine
description: A sweet and spicy twist on traditional Tunisian cuisine, featuring crispy plantains and a rich banana stew.

ingredients.name: 4 ripe bananas
ingredients.notes: Choose firm but ripe bananas for the best results.

ingredients.name: 2 large plantains
ingredients.notes: Plantains add a crispy texture to the dish.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons coconut oil
ingredients.notes: Use coconut oil for a paleo-friendly option.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon ground cumin
ingredients.notes: Cumin adds a warm, earthy flavor to the dish.

ingredients.name: 1 teaspoon smoked paprika
ingredients.notes: Smoked paprika adds a smoky depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon cayenne pepper
ingredients.notes: Use cayenne pepper for a spicy kick.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon salt
ingredients.notes: Use salt to bring out the flavors in the dish.

ingredients.name: 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
ingredients.notes: Use black pepper to add depth to the dish.

ingredients.name: 2 cloves garlic
ingredients.notes: Mince the garlic for added flavor.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup chopped fresh cilantro
ingredients.notes: Use chopped cilantro as a garnish.

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons honey
ingredients.notes: Use honey to balance out the heat in the dish.

ingredients.name: 1 cup chicken broth
ingredients.notes: Use chicken broth to add moisture to the dish.

instructions: Peel the plantains and cut them into 1-inch pieces. Place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Drizzle the coconut oil over the plantains and toss to coat. Sprinkle with salt and pepper to taste.
Bake the plantains in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until crispy and golden brown.
In a large skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the cumin, smoked paprika, cayenne pepper, and salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes, or until fragrant.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the bananas to the skillet and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until they start to soften.
Add the chicken broth to the skillet and stir to combine. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture cook for 10-15 minutes, or until the bananas are tender.
Stir in the honey and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the mixture has thickened slightly.
Serve the banana stew over the crispy plantains, garnished with chopped cilantro.
Enjoy your Spicy Crispy Tunisian Banana Tagine!
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
